texto: Nothing important in this world is measured by grades. Intelligence, character, integrity, success, happiness - do you want these things, or do you want to struggle with the arbitrary difference between an A minus and a B plus?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ryan Quinn que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “Nothing important in this world is measured by grades. Intelligence, character, integrity, succes…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre educacao, vida, school, com foco em school.

frase_original: Nothing important in this world is measured by grades. Intelligence, character, integrity, success, happiness - do you want these things, or do you want to struggle with the arbitrary difference between an A minus and a B plus?

texto: But that's life. That's your education. A series of opportunities and missed opportunities. Exams and grades and blue books and blue balls and majors and minors and liberal arts and liberal minds. The scam of it is, no matter how much you paid or how far you traveled, everybody's receipt says pretty much the same damn thing. BA, MBA, JD, PhD, MA, BS.BS. That's all it is, right?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ryan Quinn que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “But that's life. That's your education. A series of opportunities and missed opportunities. Exams…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre educacao, school, reflexao, com foco em viagem e estudos.
